finding: Identifier_Status evaluation must be version-pinned, applied after NFC normalization, and fail closed when a scalar has no parsed status. Profile status is only one upstream input to the independent identifier-admission gate.
design_implication: Keep Identifier_Status parsing and evaluation separate from confusable-skeleton generation. Reject malformed, overlapping, non-scalar, Restricted, and uncovered inputs; preserve version and source digest for downstream admission evidence.

verification.runtime: Node test runner in isolated local runtime
cases: missing status coverage rejected and marked incomplete

uncertainty: The current Unicode data endpoint was inspected as a primary source, but the artifact does not download, authenticate, or digest that remote file. Tests use synthetic format-compatible data.
trust_limit: This is not full UTS #39 conformance. It does not evaluate XID syntax, Identifier_Type exceptions, joining-control contexts, script restrictions, confusable skeletons, or dataset authenticity.
falsification_route: Provide malformed or overlapping data that parses, an uncovered or Restricted scalar that becomes eligible, canonically equivalent inputs with divergent results, or evidence that NFC is not the required canonical-equivalence boundary for this application policy.
